channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Scotland boss Clarke by those who know him best
item.description: As Steve Clarke prepares to lead Scotland at their first men's World Cup since 1998, those who know him well reveal what makes him successful.

item.title: Costs rise as Man Utd agree new $550m funding deal
item.description: The cost of servicing Manchester United's debt will rise following a new $550m deal that will see interest rates increase from 3.79% to 5.36%

item.title: Premier League target hair pulling and set-piece grappling in rules review
item.description: Premier League referees have been told to change the way they judge hair pulling after three players were sent off last season.

item.title: Watford great Jackett dies aged 64
item.description: Watford legend and former Wales international Kenny Jackett dies at the age of 64.

item.title: Scotland's McTominay 'ready to go' for Haiti opener after illness
item.description: Scotland midfielder Scott McTominay takes part in training on the eve of the World Cup opener against Haiti, having missed out the previous day because of an upset stomach.

item.title: Man City sign England's Mead on free transfer
item.description: Manchester City have confirmed the signing of England forward Beth Mead on a free transfer from Arsenal.

item.title: Critics of Mbappe have gone 'too far', says Dembele
item.description: France forward Ousmane Dembele defends his national team captain Kylian Mbappe, saying criticism of the Real Madrid striker has gone "too far".

item.title: Robbie Williams named Port Vale shirt sponsor
item.description: Robbie Williams' RW mark is appearing on Port Vale home and away shirts for the next two seasons.

item.title: 'Best Canada team ever' bid to shine at home World Cup
item.description: Amid political turbulence in the US and talk of moving matches out of Mexico, it can be forgotten that there is a third co-host of the 2026 World Cup. Canada hope it will be their time to shine.

item.title: Three red cards shown as Mexico beat South Africa in World Cup opener
item.description: Three players are sent off as World Cup co-hosts Mexico defeat South Africa in a dramatic opening match at a packed Azteca Stadium.

item.title: Dzeko's last dance could be Bosnia's new beginning
item.description: As Edin Dzeko enters the final stage of his career, BBC Sport tells the story of an underappreciated player who survived war to make history for Bosnia-Herzegovina.

item.title: You are Steve Clarke... what would you do against Haiti?
item.description: Put yourself in the shoes of Steve Clarke and pick the side, tactics and decide what your pre-match team talk would be for their World Cup opener.

item.title: Weir's title regret as she confirms Real Madrid exit
item.description: Scotland midfielder Caroline Weir has confirmed her exit from Real Madrid after four years with the La Liga runners-up without giving a hint of her next destination.

item.title: Is Shankland finally the striker Scotland have been waiting for?
item.description: Lawrence Shankland might now be in his 30s, but he is finally coming of age for Scotland, writes Tom English.

item.title: Republic of Ireland v Israel to be played at neutral venue
item.description: The Football Association of Ireland confirms the Republic of Ireland's home Nations League fixture with Israel later this year will be held at a neutral venue.

item.title: 'Nightmare from start to finish' for South Africa in opener
item.description: Former captain Dean Furman says South Africa were "miles off it" in their World Cup opener against Mexico, as many on the continent 'hate-watch' Bafana Bafana.

item.title: A new way to experience the World Cup - only on BBC Sport
item.description: Ever wanted to see a World Cup match from every angle? How about a full-pitch tactical view? Now you can on the BBC.

item.title: We need to feel love playing for England - Bellingham
item.description: England midfielder Jude Bellingham stresses the importance of needing to 'feel loved' as he looks to help the Three Lions enjoy World Cup success this summer.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game
item.description: Play BBC Sport's new World Cup predictor game to be in with the chance of winning a signed football.

item.title: The World Cup is bigger than ever - and BBC Sport has an app to match
item.description: BBC Sport are launching a new, improved app experience for the Fifa World Cup 2026

item.title: Set up BBC Sport website and app to show more Scotland
item.description: With Scotland at their first men's World Cup since 1998, BBC Sport is making it easier than ever to keep up with the national team's progress.

item.title: Quinones scores opening goal of 2026 World Cup
item.description: Mexico's Julian Quinones scores the opening goal of the 2026 World Cup against South Africa at Mexico City Stadium.

item.title: Three red cards shown in opening game of World Cup
item.description: Referee Wilton Pereira Sampaio shows red cards to South Africa's Yaya Sithole and Themba Zwane as well as Mexico's Cesar Montes during the opening match of the 2026 World Cup at the Mexico City Stadium.

item.title: Shakira among the performers at World Cup opening ceremony
item.description: Shakira was among the performers during the opening ceremony of the 2026 World Cup at the Mexico City Stadium ahead of the opening game between Mexico and South Africa.

item.title: 'We're excited to move forward' - Edwards' last interview at Wolves
item.description: In his last interview as Wolves head coach, Rob Edwards told BBC Sport he's looking forward to a "fresh start" with the club over the summer, following their relegation from the Premier League.

item.title: How much did the Messi-Ronaldo rivalry drive them both?
item.description: Focusing on two iconic celebrations, we look at how much the rivalry between Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo drove them both.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 5
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Gerrard? McInnes? What next for Rangers if Rohl leaves?
item.description: With Rangers boss Danny Rohl reportedly in talks with RB Salzburg, where might the Ibrox club turn next?

item.title: Three red cards - are referees getting tough at this World Cup?
item.description: Three players were sent off in the World Cup opener as Mexico beat South Africa 2-0. Are we about to see a World Cup littered with red cards?

item.title: 'No-one knows it's on' - NBA Finals feed US World Cup apathy
item.description: With the World Cup under way, how excited are people across the United States - and do they even know it is on?

item.title: 'Have you lost control?' - BBC sports editor asks Fifa chief Infantino
item.description: BBC sports editor Dan Roan quizzes Fifa president Gianni Infantino on the eve of the World Cup, after he told journalists to "chill" and "relax" over visa and ticketing issues.

item.title: Referee Omar Artan returns to Somalia
item.description: Referee Omar Artan arrives back in Somalia after being denied entry to the United States for the World Cup because of his "association with suspected members of terror organisations".

item.title: USA not totally reliant on Pulisic - Balboa
item.description: Former United States defender Marcelo Balboa says his country are not totally reliant on AC Milan star Christian Pulisic as the co-hosts prepare for their opening game of the World Cup.

item.title: 'Rashford's a dead cert' – Rooney, Walcott & Defoe on England's wide options
item.description: Wayne Rooney, Theo Walcott, Jermain Defoe, Kae Kurd and Kelly Somers assess England's wide options for the World Cup.

item.title: Watch: Weir scores four as Scotland beat Israel to win group
item.description: Watch the highlights as Scotland beat Israel 5-1, with Caroline Weir scoring four goals in Hungary to top their Women's World Cup qualifying group ahead of Belgium and win promotion to League A for the next Nations League.

item.title: Highlights: Wales beat Czech Republic 3-1 to top group
item.description: Watch highlights as Wales beat Czech Republic in Cardiff in Women's World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Highlights: Switzerland sweep aside Northern Ireland
item.description: Watch highlights as group winners Switzerland finish their World Cup qualifying campaign with a 2-1 win over Northern Ireland in Lurgan.

item.title: When do England play and how can you follow it?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at England's next World Cup fixture and how you can follow along via the BBC.

item.title: World Cup quiz: Can you identify these 2026 host cities?
item.description: We have selected seven of the 16 World Cup host cities for you to identify. Can you pick them out from our clues?

item.title: Any early shocks? Sutton's World Cup 2026 score predictions - opening group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the first round of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Newcastle's Hall keen on Man Utd switch - Friday's gossip
item.description: Manchester United keen on wantaway Newcastle left-back Lewis Hall, Spanish giants close in on Bernardo Silva, Real Madrid target Manchester City's Josko Gvardiol

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: From near death to World Cup hero - Jimenez's tears for Mexico
item.description: From nearly dying on the pitch to scoring for Mexico at the World Cup - Raul Jimenez has completed a remarkable comeback.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: World Cup kicks off in Mexico with Shakira, dancing, and protests
item.description: There was a star-studded opening ceremony, and some sporadic clashes between police and protesters around the Azteca stadium.

item.title: London Stadium and West Ham women's team not aware of David Sullivan contact ban
item.description: The owner of West Ham United's home has raised concerns it was not told about safeguarding restrictions.

item.title: 'You're not even the best player in your house' - the rise of 'Meatball' McGinn
item.description: John McGinn was a boy from Clydebank with a "big, round head" and bigger dreams. Scotland's World Cup opener marks a new high in a storybook career.

item.title: Messi, Maradona or Pele? Ranking the top 10 World Cup legends
item.description: Who are the biggest World Cup legends of all time? From Pele to Lionel Messi, we attempt to name the top 10.

item.title: Who will win the World Cup? BBC pundits make their predictions
item.description: Who will win, who might spring a surprise and how will England and Scotland do? BBC pundits predict what will happen at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Man City second offer worth £120m for Anderson rejected
item.description: Elliot Anderson would prefer a move to Manchester City over Manchester United should a deal be agreed with Nottingham Forest, but a second offer has been rejected.

item.title: Fatigue? The World Cup players who have had toughest seasons
item.description: BBC Sport examines which World Cup teams and players have played the most minutes since last summer, when the Club World Cup was kicking off in the United States.

item.title: Drop deep, cash in - why Kane can be England's free-scoring false nine
item.description: How has Harry Kane managed to enjoy the best goalscoring season of his career at Bayern Munich when dropping deep so often? Thomas Tuchel knows - and England might now feel the benefit.

item.title: What history tells us about Scotland's World Cup openers
item.description: With Sunday's (02:00 BST) encounter with Haiti ending a 28-year absence from football's grandest stage, BBC Scotland has been digging through the archives.

item.title: Dalglish, Souness & Rod Stewart - Scotland's World Cup '86 remembered
item.description: Remembering Scotland's 1986 World Cup campaign, their fourth successive appearance at the tournament.

item.title: Infantino could have stood up for the World Cup - but he said 'chill, relax'
item.description: Gianni Infantino faced the media for the first time in three years, but he did little to convince that Fifa has lost its grip on the organisation of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: The wild hip-hop parties that started Tuchel's journey to England boss
item.description: Thomas Tuchel is bidding to become the first foreign manager to win the World Cup. BBC Sport charts the start of his journey to becoming England boss.

item.title: 'Mazadona', 'Einstein', 'The Ant' - 20 new players to watch at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: BBC Sport TV and radio commentators pick the players they think could be the breakout stars of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Which club received most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the teams who have received the most red cards in a Premier League season.

item.title: Who won the Premier League golden boot?
item.description: BBC's Ask Me Anything team explains who has won the Premier League golden boot and other individual awards.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: Klopp role in putting Rohl in Salzburg frame - gossip
item.description: Former Liverpool boss plays role in putting Rangers head coach in frame for RB Salzburg job as Celtic backroom team asked to take wage cuts.

item.title: Man City agree deal to sign England's Charles
item.description: Manchester City have agreed a deal to sign England defender Niamh Charles from Chelsea.

item.title: National League clubs urge FA to end proposals over academy teams
item.description: Fifty two Women's National League clubs sign a letter sent to the Football Association opposing proposals to bring top tier academy teams into their league.

item.title: Wales bosses lobbying Uefa to expand Women's Euros
item.description: The Football Association of Wales wants Uefa to expand the Women's European Championship from 16 to 24 teams, in line with the men's competition.

item.title: Why are so few women reaching the top in coaching?
item.description: Hannah Dingley became the first female manager of a professional men's side in England in 2023 but she says more needs to be done to help women advance in coaching roles.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
